verdict_reasoning: HSPA5 (BiP/GRP78) is canonically an ER-luminal chaperone with no intrinsic membrane anchor. However, it is well-documented to translocate to the cell surface during cellular stress (viral infection, tumorogenesis, hypoxia, ER stress), where it becomes accessible to antibody and CAR-T targeting. Surface translocation occurs via stress-induced exocytosis and ER-PM junctional remodeling. Multiple clinical programs target surface HSPA5 on stressed / tumor cells. This qualifies as contextual surface accessibility via cell-state-induced translocation.
